title: Honeymoon murder: Meghalaya court reserves order on bail plea of key accused
content: Kushwaha is the alleged boyfriend of Raghuvanshi's wife Sonam, another accused who was granted bail on Tuesday (April 28) by the district and sessions court of Meghalaya's East Khasi district

title: Bihar govt removes Sanjay Gandhi's name from Patna zoo, dairy technology institute
content: Among the other 61 decisions taken was the allocation of ₹23,165 crore for the 'Mukhyamantri Vidyut Upbhokta Sahayta Yojana', under which power consumers in the State get 125 units of free electricity every month

title: EPFO to launch new portal to track inoperative accounts
content: E-PRAAPTI will provide an Aadhaar-based authentication mechanism through which members can securely access their old EPF account that may not have a UAN linked with it, said an official

title: NMC does away with ceiling on MBBS intake
content: The notification also removed the limit of 100 MBBS seats per 10 lakh population in a State or a Union Territory.

title: From turnout to transfers, Bengal elections marked by many firsts
content: Curtains down on second and final phase of polling; this West Bengal Assembly election emerges as most-watched State polls in recent Indian history.

title: India–Sri Lanka maritime ties strengthened through IN–SLN DIVEX 2026
content: Focused on complex underwater operations, the exercise was aimed at strengthening interoperability through advanced deep-sea diving evolutions, including mixed gas diving drills, according to the Indian Navy

title: 15 labourers die as van overturns in Madhya Pradesh’s Dhar
content: Six women and two children are among the deceased and four of the injured are in critical condition, police say

title: Kadambrayar pollution: local bodies told to step up enforcement
content: Secretaries of local bodies, including Kochi Corporation, Thrikkakara municipality, and the Edathala, Kunnathunadu and Kizhakkambalam grama panchayats, have been directed by the LSGD to impose penalties and initiate legal proceedings against those found violating norms

title: Heavy rain leaves a trail of widespread damage in Bengaluru
content: Heavy rain, thunderstorms, and hailstorms brought much-needed relief from the searing heat, but also left a trail of widespread damage, exposing how unprepared the city has been for the monsoon this year too

title: Bengaluru rain: Commuters stranded as thousands struggle to find a way back home
content: The sudden downpour triggered a sharp surge in metro ridership, with many commuters opting for the metro over road transport.

title: Pune police ask 3 U.S. nationals to leave India over violation of tourist visa norms
content: The trio, all men, was recently found distributing pamphlets of religious nature in the Sadashiv Peth area of the city

title: India, U.K. need to simplify their regulatory mechanisms: London’s Lady Mayor
content: During her maiden visit to India, Mayor Dame Susan Langley emphasised on the need for both the countries to understand each other’s markets more strategically

title: Kargil voices concern over Ladakh’s 7 new districts, ‘only 2 Muslim-majority’
content: Mustafa Haji, a lawyer and also the legal advisor of the Leh Apex Body (LAB), said the manner in which the districts have been created reflected “huge discrimination towards the Muslim population of Kargil”

title: Record 92.6% turnout in West Bengal’s phase two poll; total State turnout highest since Independence
content: Total turnout across two phases of polling in the State stands at nearly 93%; CM accuses BJP of trying to “rig” the election at her Bhabanipur constituency

title: Courts cannot reform faith, says SC in Sabarimala review
content: Religion must mend itself to stay relevant, say women who entered Kerala temple to enforce right to worship under a 2018 verdict; religious practices must harmonise with social reform, they say

title: Epic Group’s $100 million-garment manufacturing unit inaugurated in Odisha
content: Company’s core focus is net-zero carbon through solar, biomass, battery storage, and energy-efficient systems, says group founder Ranjan Mahtani

title: Delhi Excise policy case: HC adjourns to May 4 CBI plea against Kejriwal’s discharge
content: AAP leaders Arvind Kejriwal, Manish Sisodia and Durgesh Pathak have written to Delhi HC Justice Swarana Kantha Sharma, saying that they will not appear in person or through a lawyer before the Court

title: Days after logjam in BRICS envoys meeting, Jaishankar speaks with Araghchi
content: Ministers discuss current Iran-U.S. ceasefire, “bilateral, regional and international issues”; Iranian FM is yet to confirm participation in next month’s BRICS ministerial meet in Delhi; Russian FM will attend

title: Exit polls predict close contest in West Bengal, DMK+ lead in Tamil Nadu, UDF ahead in Kerala, NDA victory in Assam and Puducherry
content: Axis My India projected a huge surge for actor-turned-politician Vijay’s Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK)

title: Exit polls 2026 highlights: Higher voter turnout in favour of Trinamool Congress, says Saugata Roy
content: Reacting to the exit poll, TMC MP Saugata Roy said, "the party will do very well. High voter turnout is in favour of TMC. It is against the BJP because people are angry with the role of the Election Commission, which is in a conspiracy with the BJP."

title: Judiciary chapter ‘scrutinised at the highest level, including NCERT director,’ expert states in textbook row
content: Suparna Diwakar, one of the three experts named as ‘responsible’ by the NCERT in drafting the ‘corruption in judiciary’ chapter in now-banned Class 8 Social Science book, says she had ‘no authorial, evaluative or decision-making role’

title: Court rejects autorickshaw permit plea of man acquitted in 26/11 terror case
content: Bombay High Court says refusal of authorities to issue police clearance certificate on security grounds justified

title: Bombay High Court grants bail to Sharad Kalaskar in Narendra Dabholkar murder case
content: The court refused a request by the Central Bureau of Investigation to stay the bail order for four weeks, while noting doubts over his identification as the assailant

title: Police zero in on suspects behind morphed images of  women on social media
content: Joint investigation in progress by police in Malappuram, Kannur, Wayanad, and Kozhikode following reports that such images are being frequently traded through secret online groups

title: U.S. returns 657 stolen artefacts to India in $14 million restitution
content: Sources said the 657 antiquities were delivered in three phases: 612 were returned in November 2024, 26 in July 2025, and in the third phase, 19 were returned to India on Tuesday (April 28, 2026).

title: PM Modi inaugurates 594-km Ganga Expressway in Uttar Pradesh, praises overall development
content: Uttar Pradesh is swiftly progressing towards becoming a one-trillion-dollar economy, says the Prime Minister

title: Erattupetta municipality in Kerala’s Kottayam moves against alleged hate campaign on social media
content: Attempts being made to stoke communal tensions in the aftermath of a recent road accident in the town

title: Kerala IAS officer B. Ashok placed under suspension
content: For criticising the Kerala government's policies on social media. The 1998 batch officer is currently Vice-Chancellor, Kerala Agricultural University, and Principal Secretary, Sainik Welfare

title: India accelerates maritime expansion plan amid Strait of Hormuz concerns
content: An inter-ministerial review meeting, chaired by Union Minister of Ports, Shipping and Waterways Sarbananda Sonowal, focused on building a robust and future-ready maritime ecosystem to safeguard national supply chains

title: Hate speech stems from an ‘us versus them’ mindset: Supreme Court judgment
content: Hate speech is not merely a deviation from acceptable discourse; it is fundamentally antithetical to the constitutional value of fraternity and strikes at the moral fabric of our Republic, SC says

title: All government services in Andhra Pradesh to go online by December
content: Emphasising the expansion of ‘WhatsApp Governance’ to all public services, Chief Minister Naidu says the move aims to eliminate the need for citizens to do the rounds of government offices

title: Exit polls: DMK in TN, BJP in Assam, UDF in Kerala? | Above the Fold | 29.04.2026
content: From exit polls across five States, Phase 2 polling in West Bengal to the UAE’s OPEC exit and Rahul Gandhi’s take on the Great Nicobar project - We bring you the top stories of the day.

title: Andhra Pradesh rolls out Marine ABS framework for fishermen
content: APSBB Chairman N. Vijay Kumar says initiative will channel marine resource revenues to fishermen while promoting biodiversity conservation

title: T.N. Commission for Protection of Child Rights recommends legal action against Vijay for using children to ‘influence electoral outcome’
content: TNCPCR’s recommendation followed a representation by Tamil Nadu Child Rights Watch, which raised serious concerns on “emotional exploitation of children for electoral influence”

title: Telangana Govt signs ₹1,461 crore deal to take over Hyderabad Metro Rail Phase I
content: The acquisition of Hyderabad Metro Rail Phase I is expected to enable closer strategic alignment between the existing network and the proposed Phase II

title: Exit polls differ on West Bengal, Tamil Nadu; give Assam to BJP, Kerala to Cong
content: Exit polls suggest a BJP-led alliance victory in Assam and a UDF win in Kerala, potentially leaving the Left out of power in any state. Puducherry is expected to see the AINRC-BJP combine retain power. Tamil Nadu and West Bengal present a mixed picture, with some polls indicating a tight race in Tamil Nadu and differing predictions for West Bengal.

title: 92.6% turnout in Phase 2 takes Bengal total to record 92.9%
content: West Bengal witnessed an unprecedented voter turnout of 92.9% across two phases, surpassing previous records and nearing India's highest. This surge, particularly in Kolkata, is attributed to voter roll corrections and enhanced security. Despite a reduced electorate, more votes were cast, with women outvoting men. Officials are investigating complaints of vote tampering.

title: IPL: SRH chase 244! Head, Klaasen overshadow Rickelton’s historic MI ton in run-fest
content: Travis Head and Heinrich Klaasen powered Sunrisers Hyderabad to a six-wicket victory over Mumbai Indians, successfully chasing a massive 244 with eight balls to spare. Their explosive batting overshadowed Ryan Rickelton's record-breaking unbeaten 123, which had propelled Mumbai to their highest total batting first. SRH's remarkable chase, the fourth-highest in IPL history, saw them reach 249/4.

title: Justice dept sues $5B tech firm for excluding Americans from applying to high-paying jobs
content: The US Department of Justice is suing tech firm Cloudera for allegedly discriminating against American job seekers. Prosecutors claim the company created a sham hiring process, favoring temporary visa holders for lucrative roles and actively deterring US workers. This lawsuit highlights the DOJ's commitment to protecting domestic employment opportunities.

title: After $6.25bn 'gift' to 'Trump accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750m
content: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.

title: Bengal polls: Paths of 'Singham' & 'Pushpa' don't cross, Falta focuses on taped EVM
content: Falta constituency witnessed a high-stakes election day with IPS Ajay Pal Sharma, dubbed 'Singham', patrolling while TMC candidate Jahangir Khan, 'Pushpa', monitored from his control room. Despite charged rhetoric, the anticipated confrontation didn't materialize, overshadowed by EVM complaints, leading to a repoll in Falta.

title: Google withdraws from Pentagon's $100 million drone contest
content: Google quietly withdrew from a Pentagon competition to build voice-controlled autonomous drone swarms—after initially being selected as a successful submission. The $100 million contest, run by Special Operations Command and the Defense Innovation Unit, seeks technology to direct drone swarms in real-time battlefield scenarios. Google cited "resourcing" issues, but the exit is said to be  followed by an internal ethics review. OpenAI, Palantir, and xAI remain in the running.

title: Record turnout over two phases harbinger of change in Bengal: Modi
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi stated that Bengal's record voter turnout signals a change, expressing confidence in the BJP's victory. He also predicted NDA's retention of Assam and Puducherry, with potential surprises in Kerala and Tamil Nadu. Modi highlighted the high turnout as a reflection of fear-free elections and voters' awareness of their democratic rights.

title: Steph & Ayesha Curry join Warren Buffett for iconic $50M+ charity lunch revival
content: Stephen Curry, the Golden State Warriors’ legend, and his wife, Ayesha Curry, have always been passionate about helping the underprivileged through the success they have achieved in their lives and their wealth. The high profile couple’s foundation, Eat. Learn. Play. has always been devoted to the children of Oakland and their betterment.

title: Luka Doncic injury update: doctor sets 10-14 day return timeline for Lakers star
content: The latest Luka Doncic injury update points to steady progress as he resumes on-court movement following a hamstring strain. While coach JJ Redick remains cautious, medical insight suggests a possible return in early May. Doncic is expected to miss the rest of the first-round series against the Rockets, where the Lakers hold a 3-1 lead. Game 5 brings fresh lineup shifts, with Austin Reaves set to return and Kevin Durant ruled out, shaping a pivotal playoff moment.

title: Jordon Hudson fuels Russini-Vrabel controversy with conspiracy claim
content: The Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel controversy has taken a new turn after Jordon Hudson reacted to a viral theory linking Chris Simms’ NBC exit to the situation. While some believe Simms knew inside details, others say his removal was part of a planned lineup change. With no clear proof yet, the debate continues to grow.

title: SIR divides families across Kolkata: Chosen few celebrate vote as lakhs mourn deletion
content: Kolkata's special electoral roll revision left many families fractured on poll day. While some voted, lakhs were disenfranchised as their names were deleted.  Instances like Mariam Zaki, whose father and grandfather couldn't vote, and former MP Tarun Mandal highlight the widespread disappointment and confusion caused by the process, leaving many questioning the fairness of the deletions.

title: Iran 'choking like a stuffed pig': Trump digs in on Hormuz blockade
content: United States President Donald Trump states the naval blockade on Iran will continue until Tehran agrees to a nuclear deal.  Military strikes are also being planned.  Iran warns of unprecedented action if pressure persists.  The situation is volatile, with potential for negotiation or confrontation.

title: TN exit polls: AK breakthrough or PK's 'farsh' moment — what will it be for Vijay?
content: Exit polls present a stark contrast for Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam in the 2026 Tamil Nadu elections. While most predict a spoiler role with 10-24 seats, one outlier suggests a Kejriwal-like breakthrough of 98-120 seats. The outcome hinges on converting star power into grassroots strength, unlike Prashant Kishor's Bihar debut.

title: IPL 2026 Orange Cap: Abhishek, Klaasen surge ahead of Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: Abhishek Sharma has regained the Orange Cap with a swift 45, surpassing Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. Sharma now boasts 425 runs, with teammate Heinrich Klaasen climbing to second. Sooryavanshi drops to third, while KL Rahul and Virat Kohli round out the top five in a dynamic run-scoring race.

title: Federal Reserve keeps interest rates unchanged, cites Middle East conflicts uncertainty
content: US Fed Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meet: Jerome Powell-led US Federal Reserve on Wednesday kept the interest rates unchanged in the 3.5-3.75% range citing upside risks to inflation in view of the rising global energy prices.

title: Putin, Trump hold 90-minute call on Iran, Ukraine war: Russia proposes Kyiv ceasefire
content: Presidents Putin and Trump held a 1.5-hour call discussing the Middle East war and Ukraine. Putin backed Trump's Iran ceasefire extension, viewing it as a stabilizing move. He also proposed a temporary ceasefire in Ukraine for Victory Day, aiming to advance peace negotiations.

title: IPL 2026 Points Table: SRH storm into top three, MI slump continues
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad achieved a remarkable six-wicket win against Mumbai Indians. They successfully chased a record 244 runs at the Wankhede Stadium. This victory marks their fifth consecutive win in IPL 2026. Sunrisers Hyderabad now move to third place in the points table. Mumbai Indians face their sixth loss this season.

title: Abhishek Sharma-Travis Head create IPL history with blazing stand vs MI
content: Mumbai Indians posted a huge 243/5 thanks to Ryan Rickelton's 123.  Chasing this target, Abhishek Sharma and Travis Head formed a record opening partnership.  Their 129-run stand put Sunrisers Hyderabad in a strong position.  This partnership now equals the record for most century stands for the opening wicket in IPL history.

title: Bengal logs record 91.66% turnout in phase 2, sporadic violence reported across districts
content: West Bengal witnessed a record voter turnout exceeding 91% in the second phase of assembly elections, contributing to an overall 92.47% across two phases, the highest since Independence. Despite high participation, sporadic incidents of violence and vandalism were reported from various locations, prompting the Election Commission to seek reports and ensure fair polling.

title: Kerala exit polls: UDF predicted to win coastal state in neck-and-neck contest with LDF
content: A defeat for LDF would also diminish the presence of CPM in its last major stronghold. The campaign narrative has also undergone notable shifts. Early phases were dominated by issues such as corruption allegations, economic concerns, backdoor appointments, the Sabarimala gold heist controversy, and rehabilitation concerns of Wayanad landslide victims, alongside scandals involving opposition leaders.

title: Man charged in Trump assassination bid took photo with knife before assault
content: A California man, Cole Allen, is accused of attempting to assassinate President Trump at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner. Prosecutors revealed he photographed himself armed before the attack, which involved firing a shotgun at security. Allen, who had planned his actions, faces multiple charges.

title: Lamine Yamal Injury: Will Barcelona star miss World Cup opener?
content: Spain plans to carefully manage Lamine Yamal’s return for the 2026 World Cup after his hamstring injury. He may miss the first game, play limited minutes in the second, and aim for full fitness by the final group match. Barcelona fully supports this cautious plan to avoid another injury setback.

title: Fastest & highest ever! Ryan Rickelton scripts MI history with maiden IPL hundred
content: South African opener Ryan Rickelton rewrote Mumbai Indians' record books with a blistering 123 not out off 55 balls, the fastest IPL century for the franchise in just 44 deliveries. His exceptional knock powered MI to a massive 243/5 against Sunrisers Hyderabad at the Wankhede, marking a historic individual performance.

title: ICSE ISC result 2026 to be declared tomorrow: When & where to check 10th, 12th scorecards
content: The Council for the Indian School Certificate Examinations (CISCE) has announced that ICSE (Class X) and ISC (Class XII) Year 2026 Main Examination results will be released on April 30, 2026, at 11:00 AM. Students can access their scorecards on cisce.org and results.cisce.org by entering their Unique ID and Index Number.

title: Bengaluru rains: 7 dead after hospital compound wall collapses
content: Tragedy struck Bengaluru as a compound wall collapse near Bowring Hospital claimed seven lives, including three children. Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah visited the site and confirmed the fatalities, announcing Rs 5 lakh compensation for the bereaved families. Deputy Chief Minister D. K. Shivakumar also rushed to the scene to assess the situation and offer assistance.

title: Assam exit polls 2026: Himanta-led BJP projected to retain govt with strong lead
content: Exit polls for the Assam elections indicate a strong victory for the incumbent Himanta Biswa Sarma-led BJP government, projecting a third consecutive term. Most pollsters suggest the BJP will secure a comfortable majority, leaving the Congress campaign with limited gains and pushing the party further into vulnerability.

title: 'Smacks Australia's best fast bowlers': Pathan in awe of Sooryavanshi
content: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is dominating IPL 2026, leading the Orange Cap race with 400 runs. His fearless approach against top international bowlers like Hazlewood and Cummins, including hitting boundaries from the first ball, signals a new era of confidence in Indian cricket, as noted by Irfan Pathan.

title: Tamil Nadu exit polls: 3 out of 4 pollsters back Stalin return; Vijay's notable debut
content: Exit polls suggest Chief Minister MK Stalin's DMK alliance is poised to retain power in Tamil Nadu, though with a reduced majority. Newcomer Vijay's TVK shows potential to influence traditional vote bases, with projections varying but indicating a notable debut. The AIADMK faces persistent challenges, while the DMK aims for a significant milestone of consecutive terms.

title: Why Mumbai Indians players are wearing black armbands vs Sunrisers Hyderabad
content: Mumbai Indians players wore black armbands in their IPL 2026 match against Sunrisers Hyderabad to mourn the loss of a support staff member's daughter. Skipper Hardik Pandya won the toss and chose to bat, while Rohit Sharma remains sidelined due to recovery. SRH captain Pat Cummins anticipated a challenging pitch.

title: Puducherry exit polls: NDA likely to retain power; Congress, TVK lag in fragmented fight
content: Puducherry's 2026 elections saw a massive 89.83% voter turnout, with exit polls suggesting a tight race. The ruling NDA, led by AINRC-BJP, appears to have a slight edge over the Congress-DMK alliance. A new force, Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam, has also made its presence felt in several key constituencies, potentially fragmenting the results.

title: MI vs SRH: No Rohit Sharma again! Hardik Pandya gives big comeback update
content: Mumbai Indians face further setbacks as Rohit Sharma and Quinton de Kock are unavailable for their IPL clash against Sunrisers Hyderabad. Skipper Hardik Pandya confirmed Rohit needs more time to recover, while de Kock suffered a wrist injury. Despite these disruptions, Pandya expressed confidence in his team's preparation and opted to bat first.

title: Former ICC umpire reacts to Riyan Parag’s 'vaping' row, says 'boys should be careful'
content: Rajasthan Royals player Riyan Parag was reportedly seen vaping in the dressing room during a match, sparking debate and drawing a stern warning from former umpire Anil Chaudhary. The incident highlights concerns over player conduct and potential violations of India's ban on e-cigarettes, despite no specific cricket rule against vaping.

title: ‘AI chip in bat’? Vaibhav Sooryavanshi breaks silence with cheeky reply – Watch
content: Rajasthan Royals' 15-year-old sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi humorously dismissed 'AI chip' allegations after a record-breaking IPL 2026 performance.  He cheekily attributed his success to divine intervention.  Sooryavanshi also became the fastest to 400 runs this season, showcasing his extraordinary talent and fearless batting.

title: 'Better get smart soon!' Trump tells Iran to 'get their act together'; shares AI image with a gun
content: President Trump criticized Iran's negotiation tactics, stating they "don't know how to sign a non-nuclear deal" and urged them to "get smart soon." This comes as the US reportedly prepares for an extended blockade strategy to limit Iran's regional influence, shifting from short-term measures to sustained economic and military pressure.

title: 'We are all scared right now': ICE arrests Indian-origin truck driver in Oregon crash case
content: ICE has arrested an Indian-origin truck driver, Rajinder Kumar, accused in a fatal 2025 Oregon road accident that killed a newly married couple. Released from local custody on April 2, Kumar now faces removal proceedings after ICE claims local authorities did not comply with a detainer request.

title: Trump Says US Has "A Shot" At Crewed Moon Landing Before Presidency Ends
content: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday said he believes NASA has a good chance of returning astronauts to the Moon's surface before he leaves the White House at the start of 2029.

title: Alleged Trump Assassin Took Selfie Moments Before Attack: Prosecutors
content: The man accused of trying to assassinate President Donald Trump took a selfie in his hotel room moments before bursting through security with a pump-action shotgun, prosecutors said Wednesday.

title: "I Literally Was A Fool": Elon Musk Grilled In OpenAI Trial
content: Elon Musk faced fiery questioning Wednesday in his court showdown with OpenAI, as he insisted that the maker of ChatGPT had fooled him by turning what was an altruistic pursuit into a profit-making juggernaut.

title: Bengal Voters Staying Mum, May Not Release Exit Poll Data Today: Axis My India Founder
content: Some surveys forecast a victory for the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP), while others suggest that the Trinamool Congress (TMC), led by Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee, will retain power.

title: Pakistan Facing Major Economic Fallout Due To Iran War, Says PM Sharif
content: Pakistan's oil import bill had surged from $300 million before the conflict to $800 million, the prime minister said.

title: US Supreme Court Undermines Key Provision Of Voting Rights Act
content: The lower court found that the map was guided too much by racial considerations in violation of the constitutional promise of equal protection under the law.

title: Bengal Sees 92.47% Voter Turnout, Highest Since Independence
content: As of 7.45 pm, poll participation in West Bengal in Phase II of the Assembly elections stood at 91.66 per cent.

title: 'Can Draw Attention, Can't Compel Legislature': Supreme Court On Hate-Speech Legal Framework
content: A bench of Justices Vikram Nath and Sandeep Mehta said it was well settled that formulation of policy and the choice of legislative response fall squarely within the domain of the legislature.

title: 2026 Poll Of Exit Polls Highlights: BJP Has Edge In Bengal, Assam, UDF Set To Win Kerala
content: Poll of Exit Poll Results 2026: Once voting concludes, the focus will quickly shift to exit poll predictions for the five states and Union Territory begin to emerge.

title: NDTV Exit Poll Highlights: TVK Shock, NDA Sweep In Assam, LDF Upset In Kerala: Axis My India Exit Poll
content: Assembly elections exit poll live updates: The weeks-long voting process saw the participation of 17 crore voters across 824 assembly constituencies.

title: Trump Says Iran "Choking Like Stuffed Pig", Refuses To Lift Hormuz Blockade: Report
content: Speaking to Axios, Trump said that the blockade is "somewhat more effective than the bombing", and as of Tuesday night, he had not ordered any kinetic action.

title: US Has Spent $25 Billion So Far In War With Iran: Pentagon Official
content: This is the first official estimate of the military's price tag for the war that started after the US and Israel launched missile and drone attacks

title: 140 Chickens Die After Wedding DJ Decides To Crank Up The Bass
content: A poultry farm owner in Uttar Pradesh has filed a police complaint after 140 chickens died at his farm, alleging that the cause was loud music from a DJ system used during a wedding procession that passed nearby on the night of April 25.

title: Faridabad Student Dies By Suicide. He Was Stressed Over Online Gaming Loss
content: Aditya Bhati, a third-year BSc student at YMCA University, Faridabad, had lost a lot of money in online gaming.

title: Trump, Putin Discuss Iran War And Ukraine In Over 90-Minute Call
content: The leaders also discussed the war in Ukraine, now in its fifth year following Russia's invasion of its neighbour in 2022.

title: 12 Dead, 20 Injured As Van Crashes Into SUV In Madhya Pradesh
content: 35 labourers were returning home in a pickup van from Baggad in Dhar district when the driver lost control of the vehicle.

title: West Bengal Exit Poll Results 2026 Highlights: BJP To Win Bengal, Predict 4 Exit Polls
content: West Bengal Exit Poll 2026 LIVE Updates: Voters queued up from 7 AM outside booths in Kolkata, Howrah, Hooghly, Nadia, North and South 24 Parganas and Purba Bardhaman districts, which form Bengal's electoral and political core.

title: Bengal Elections Phase 2 Voting Highlights: Bengal Records 92.47% Voter Turnout In 2-Phase Elections
content: Bengal Assembly Elections 2026 Phase 2 Voting LIVE Updates: The second phase of voting for the intense BJP vs Trinamool battle will begin shortly.

title: Iran Foreign Minister Araghchi Dials S Jaishankar, Discusses "Ceasefire Developments"
content: The phone conversation came two days after Araghchi held wide-ranging discussions with Russian President Vladimir Putin in Moscow.

title: Air India may make meals optional on domestic and short international flights
content: Determine the monthly installment amount for a loan Estimate the returns on investments made through SIPs Find out maturity amount and interest earned on PPF Check maturity amount and interest earned on an FD Estimate the pension amount and corpus accumulated under NPS A Mutual Fund Calculator helps estimate the future value of investments

title: 'Will be lifeline of UP's development': PM Modi inaugurates Ganga Expressway
content: the 594-km greenfield corridor connecting Prayagraj and Meerut will be extended to Haridwar in Uttarakhand . govt will also work on a link eway to Farrukhabad and connect it with other eways . the project symbolises the blending of "vikas" and "virasat"

title: West Bengal polls: Scuffles, plaints mark election debut of RG Kar victim’s mother
content: RG Kar rape-and-murder victim's mother was her first poll day as a candidate . she and her husband cast their votes at Natagarh Sangha around 7.20 am, close to their home . the morning began on a personal note, and she said, "this morning, I entered my daughter's room"

title: UAE's Opec exit, decision to up crude output could help India
content: Atul Mathur is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India with over 27 years of experience in journalism. Based in Delhi, he has spent much of his career reporting on governance, public policy and politics, churning out researched, data-driven stories that impact daily lives. Atul is known for investigative depth and strong human-interest narratives as he strives to bring clarity and context to complex issues. He currently tracks the energy sector, writing on power, renewable energy, coal and mines. title: Bengal polls: VIP treatment for 104-year-old who fought SIR & won
content: the 104-year-old has cast his vote in every election since independence . the EC had initially placed his name under adjudication on the final voters' list . after days of anxiety and uncertainty, his name eventually appeared on the first supplementary list, but the delay meant he missed the vote-from-home window for 85-plus citizens .

title: India's first indigenous air-launched anti-ship missile succesfully tested
content: defence research & development organisation launched anti-ship missile-short range (NASM-SR) from a helicopter platform in Bay of Bengal off Odisha coast . two missiles were launched in quick succession from the same helicopter, making it the first salvo launch of an advanced air-launched missile system . the missiles demonstrated wat-and-forget operation in all weather conditions and at all hours .

title: Araghchi dials Jaishankar to discuss West Asia situation
content: s Jaishankar says he received a call from his foreign minister . he said he had a detailed conversation about various aspects of the current situation . this was the sixth conversation between the two foreign ministers since the start of the west Asia conflict on febr 28 .
